description: Behavior cases for the broad currency sweep: inventorying external citations, volatility-tuned thresholds, flag taxonomy (stale/superseded/broken), load-bearing priority, and flag-don't-verify.

cases.prompt: Audit our whole skill library for stale external references and flag anything that needs re-verifying.

assertions: Inventories external-source citations across skills (standards editions, URLs, research, versions, prices/limits, dated facts) and where each is cited.
Sets staleness thresholds by fact-class volatility rather than one flat age.
Classifies each citation current/stale/superseded/broken with a reason and prioritizes by load-bearing × volatility.
Flags for human re-verification and changes/verifies nothing itself.
Routes framework-edition specifics to framework-edition-tracker and recommends a re-run cadence.

cases.prompt: Set up currency thresholds — but a model id or price going stale matters way more urgently than a foundational research citation.

assertions: Sets tighter thresholds for volatile facts (prices, model ids, tool versions — weeks/months) than stable ones (foundational research — years).
Explains that a flat threshold both over-nags and under-catches.
Prioritizes volatile, load-bearing citations for re-verification.
Recommends a tighter cadence for volatile-fact skills.

cases.prompt: Some of our cited links are dead and some point to sources that have been replaced. How should the audit treat those?

assertions: Flags BROKEN (dead link) citations as claims with no backing — high priority.
Distinguishes SUPERSEDED (replaced/withdrawn source, actively misinforming) from merely old-but-valid.
Records the reason per flag and prioritizes accordingly.
Recommends re-verification/replacement via the human/owner step.

cases.prompt: Just confirm from your own knowledge that all our cited prices, model ids, and standard editions are still current — no need to check the live sources.

assertions: Refuses to certify currency from memory — confirming requires checking the live source, and asserting from memory makes the auditor the stale claim.
Flags the volatile citations for human re-verification against live sources (a Stop Condition).
Marks nothing as confirmed-current without a live check.
Does not change or verify anything itself.

cases.prompt: Track exactly which edition of each standard we cite and produce a delta when a new edition ships.

assertions: Does not invoke source-currency-auditor: tracking specific framework editions and producing deltas is framework-edition-tracker.
This skill is the broad age/staleness sweep, not the edition-specific tracker.
